---
title: "Metagenome Function"
author: "Ryan Waln"
format: html
editor: visual
---
## Introduction

This file serves as a set of notes on the commands used for workshop 3 for Module 3 in MB5370 in order to assemble a metagenome of five bacterial species

The following steps are taken to assemble the metagenome:
- Assembly
- Binning w/metabat
- Bin quality check w/checkm
- Selecting bins based on checkm results
- Taxonomic classification with gtdbtk
- Finding and annotating genes with prokka
- Infering metabolic pathways

## Assembly 
### Create a directory called flye and make a symbolic link to bbd read data 
ln -s /pvol/data/metagenome_assembly/bbd_0.1.fastq.gz .

### Create A shell script named run_flye_bbd.sh in flye directory w/provided code then submit the job to slurm and track output to ensure it is running
sbatch run_flye_bbd.sh
tail -f slurm-xxx.out
